videos.title: WTF Is the Context Layer? The Missing Infrastructure for Production Agents — Prukalpa Sankar
videos.summary: This video explores the concept of the 'context layer' in AI, explaining why cognitive intelligence alone doesn't guarantee real-world effectiveness and how businesses can help AI build crucial context. By tracing the journey of a data analyst, the speaker highlights the importance of knowledge, expertise, and norms in decision-making, offering valuable insights into engineering the future of AI. It's a must-watch for anyone looking to understand how to make AI systems more useful, secure, and sustainable in enterprise environments.

timestamps.topic: Why AI models are getting smarter but not more useful
The formula for real-world performance: Intelligence vs. Context
How humans use knowledge, expertise, and norms to solve problems

videos.title: Don't Ship Skills Without Evals — Philipp Schmid, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: This video is a must-watch for AI developers and engineers looking to build highly reliable agent capabilities without falling into the trap of untested 'skills'. The speaker, Philipp Schmid, a Staff Engineer at Google DeepMind, reveals why 'vibe checks' fail in production and provides actionable best practices for creating, testing, and evaluating AI skills. The core takeaway is that developers must implement structured evals and ablation testing to ensure their agents actually improve performance rather than quietly corrupting outputs.

timestamps.topic: Agents We Use vs. Agents We Build: The Reliability Gap
Capability vs. Preference Skills & Knowing When to Retire Them
Why Self-Generated and Bloated Skills Fail

videos.title: Forward Deployed Engineering at Cursor — Pauline Brunet
videos.summary: This talk by Pauline Brunet, Head of Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) at Cursor, provides a masterful blueprint for scaling enterprise AI deployments through specialized engineering. It is a must-watch for tech leaders looking to bridge the gap between complex AI products and real-world business value, demonstrating how to align custom engineering with client maturity to maximize return on investment.

timestamps.topic: Determining FDE fit based on client digital maturity and product customization
Anatomy of a Forward Deployed Engineer: technical depth meets high EQ
How Cursor structures project-based, high-impact FDE engagements

videos.title: A hostage negotiator's guide to being conflict-ready | Ryan Dunlap | TEDxPortland
videos.summary: This engaging talk by former hostage negotiator Ryan Dunlap challenges the common notion of "healthy conflict," arguing instead that conflict is a neutral mirror reflecting the health of the individuals involved. Dunlap shares practical lessons from law enforcement and personal life to help viewers build their capacity to navigate pressure and resolve conflicts effectively. It is a must-watch for anyone looking to improve their communication skills and emotional resilience in difficult conversations.

timestamps.topic: Conflict is a Neutral Mirror
Negotiation at Work vs. at Home
Three Lessons Learned for Conflict Capacity

videos.title: AI is shaping war—what about peace? | Branka Panic | TEDxCalle Aldama
videos.summary: This thought-provoking TEDx talk by Branka Panic explores the dual nature of artificial intelligence in global conflicts, shifting the focus from fear of rogue machines to the human decisions behind them. By sharing compelling real-world examples, she demonstrates how AI can either be weaponized to accelerate devastation or utilized by peacebuilders to prevent violence, document war crimes, and foster reconciliation. It is an essential watch for anyone interested in how we can actively steer emerging technology to protect human rights and build a more peaceful future.

timestamps.topic: How AI is currently weaponized in conflict and why it is fundamentally a human problem
Branka's personal story of growing up amidst war in the Balkans and becoming a peacebuilder
The potential for AI to act as an ally for peace and human rights documentation
How AI is facilitating anonymous dialogues and inclusive peace processes in conflict zones

videos.title: What’s going wrong with this Kotlin code?
videos.summary: This quick Kotlin developer challenge presents a real-world coding puzzle: despite two strings looking identical and matching in logs, a duplicate check fails, letting duplicate requests through. Watch as the presenter explains the code setup and challenges viewers to identify the subtle bug in the comments.

timestamps.topic: Introduction to the Kotlin duplicate ID challenge
Reviewing the code and identifying the failing check

videos.title: Whats your go-to prompting hack?
videos.summary: This video is a goldmine for anyone looking to master prompt engineering, featuring five quick, practical tips from experts on how to get the best results from AI. From breaking down complex tasks to treating the AI like a human coworker, these actionable strategies will immediately improve your productivity and save you time. The core takeaway is that effective prompting isn't about complex code, but about clear communication and strategic planning.

timestamps.topic: Using LLMs to help craft and refine your prompts
Breaking large prompts down into smaller, manageable tasks

videos.title: Build With AI 2026 Recap
videos.summary: This video highlights Google’s 'Build with AI' global events, showcasing how developers and tech enthusiasts worldwide are coming together to learn and build future-proof solutions. It is an inspiring look at the power of community-driven innovation and how AI tools can be harnessed to solve real-world problems. The core takeaway is that anyone, from beginners to experts, can join developer groups and start shaping the future of AI technology.

timestamps.topic: The excitement of seeing what people will build with AI
How the events empower developers of all skill levels with AI tools
The massive global impact: 200k+ developers trained across 114 countries

videos.title: The next generation of voting reform | Sara Wolk | TEDxMtHood
videos.summary: This engaging TEDx talk explores the systemic flaws of traditional voting and why popular reforms like Ranked Choice Voting often fall short of their promises. Speaker Sara Wolk presents a compelling case for STAR Voting (Score-Then-Automatic-Runoff), a modern, highly accurate alternative designed to eliminate vote-splitting and empower voters to express their true preferences. By understanding the mathematics behind how we cast ballots, viewers will learn how a simple change in ballot design can radically improve democratic representation and accountability.

timestamps.topic: The limits of activism and the necessity of voting reform
The problem of vote-splitting in choose-one voting systems
How Ranked Choice Voting works and its hidden flaws
Comparing voting method accuracy and why STAR Voting tops the charts
An explanation of STAR Voting: Score-Then-Automatic-Runoff

videos.title: 4 things I’ve learned as a junior software engineer
videos.summary: This video offers realistic, relatable advice for junior software developers navigating their first couple of years in the tech industry. It breaks down the mental barriers that hold back new engineers, such as fear of asking questions and imposter syndrome, while emphasizing that mistakes are a normal part of the learning process. The core takeaway is that communication, adaptability, and emotional resilience are just as vital to a junior developer's success as writing clean code.

timestamps.topic: Overcoming the fear of asking questions as a junior developer
Making mistakes, handling pull request feedback, and prioritizing quality
The importance of soft skills and building team relationships

videos.title: How To Grow As a Junior Developer To Senior | 5 Tips
videos.summary: This video breaks down the five essential steps required to transition from a junior developer to a mid- or senior-level role, focusing on the often-overlooked balance between technical prowess and interpersonal growth. The core takeaway is that advancing your career isn't just about coding faster; it's about developing soft skills, learning the broader tech ecosystem, and actively stepping up as a confident decision-maker within your team.

timestamps.topic: Why soft skills are crucial for seniority
Speaking solutions instead of presenting problems
Expanding your view of the developer ecosystem

videos.title: Kerajaan U-turn Lindung 24 Jam, rakyat menang atau rugi?
videos.summary: This video breaks down the controversial 'Lindung 24 Jam' PERKESO insurance scheme, explaining why the Malaysian government initially made it mandatory before making a sudden U-turn due to public backlash. It offers valuable insights into how public pressure can influence government policy and provides a clear explanation of how the scheme actually benefits workers who lack personal medical insurance. The core takeaway is that while the scheme offers important safety nets, better public education and transparent communication are essential before implementing such policies.

timestamps.topic: How the 'Lindung 24 Jam' scheme differs from existing PERKESO coverages
Why the scheme is highly beneficial for workers without private medical cards
The government's critical failure to educate and engage the public before implementation

videos.title: “The Malaysian government might be doing its job too well…” - World Bank Lead Economist
videos.summary: In this engaging episode of BFM's Breakfast Grille, Dr. Apurva Sanghi, Lead Economist for the World Bank Group in Malaysia, breaks down how recent geopolitical conflicts and supply chain crises are reshaping both the global and Malaysian economies. He offers crucial insights on why the current crisis differs from the oil shocks of the 1970s and explores the balance between trade openness and economic vulnerability. This discussion is a must-watch for anyone wanting to understand the real-world impact of global disruptions on local markets and living standards.

timestamps.topic: How the Middle East conflict has shaped global growth forecasts and why it differs from the 1970s oil shocks
The reality of a supply-side crisis and Malaysia's deep dependency on Gulf-state commodities beyond oil
The economic impact of fuel subsidies and the trade-off between present-day public relief and future fiscal health

videos.title: What Happens When You Say “Astaghfirullah”
videos.summary: This video delves into the beautiful linguistic roots of the Arabic word 'maghfirah' (forgiveness), explaining that it means both to cover and protect, much like armor. It offers a comforting and profound Islamic perspective on how God's forgiveness shields believers in both this life and the next, transforming past mistakes into a source of protection. This is a must-watch for anyone seeking a deeper, more hopeful understanding of divine mercy and the layers of forgiveness in Islam.

timestamps.topic: The root meaning of maghfirah (forgiveness)
How forgiveness acts as protective armor
The layers of divine forgiveness: Al-Ghaafir and Al-Ghaffar

videos.title: The Best Routine (Explained by a 23yo Muslim CEO)
videos.summary: This video offers a fascinating behind-the-scenes look at how a productive Muslim CEO structures his daily routine around the five obligatory prayers while managing three companies. By focusing on sleep quality, prioritizing deep work in the early mornings, and practicing intermittent fasting (OMAD), he provides a practical blueprint for balancing worldly success with spiritual devotion. It is a must-watch for anyone looking to optimize their daily schedule using a values-driven approach to time management.

timestamps.topic: The 3-2-1 Rule for Better Sleep
The Importance of Distraction-Free Deep Work
The Benefits of Intermittent Fasting (OMAD)
